relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Aviv returns Israel to the night of liberation.
Passover gathers at the place of the Name.
Bread of affliction keeps haste and slavery in memory.
The feast refuses stale leftovers of the first night.
Passover is not scattered into private local ritual.
Sunset recalls the appointed hour of departure.
The people eat and then return to ordinary dwelling.
The seventh day closes in solemn rest.
Seven weeks begin with the first sickle in grain.
Weeks becomes a freewill gift measured by blessing.
Joy widens to children, workers, Levite, immigrant, orphan, and widow.
Slavery remembered becomes inclusion practiced.
Booths begins when grain and wine are gathered.
Harvest joy again includes those most easily left out.
Seven days of celebration culminate in wholehearted joy.
Three yearly appearances gather the people before YHWH.
The gift is proportionate to what each hand has received.
Every gate receives judges and officers.
Justice must resist partiality and bribery.
Justice must be pursued again and again because life depends upon it.
The altar may not absorb the sacred tree traditions around it.
The standing pillar is also refused within YHWH's worship.

contemplative_note: Deuteronomy 16 places festivals and courts side by side. Joy without justice is incomplete, and justice without remembered liberation becomes cold administration. The chapter asks for both: celebrate together, then judge without partiality.
